title: aquilegia flabellata f. pumila
intro: Aquilegia flabellata f. pumila, also known as the dwarf fan-leaved columbine or dwarf fan-leaved aquilegia, is a compact, low-growing perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia flabellata f. pumila include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
